_note: Status 'mapped' means the component has been mapped to this criterion via analysis. It does not mean the criterion has been verified by a formal audit. Run audit-rules.json checks and update validation_status before changing overall_status.

criteria.title: Non-text Content
criteria.evidence: Icon and arrow slots carry aria-hidden='true'. They are decorative. The accessible name of Dropdown affix is provided by the visible text in .input-affix__text.

criteria.title: Contrast (Minimum)
criteria.evidence: Default text (#161616) on Solid Default background (#F3F4F6) = ~13:1. Placeholder text (#6C737F) on Solid Default (#F3F4F6) = ~4.63:1. Selected text (#FFF) on selected background (#384250) = ~9.7:1. Disabled text (#9DA4AE) on disabled background (#E5E7EB) = ~2.1:1 — exempt per WCAG 1.4.3 for inactive UI components.

criteria.title: Keyboard
criteria.evidence: Dropdown type uses native <button> element — keyboard operable by default (Enter and Space). Text type uses <div> — non-interactive, no keyboard interaction required.

criteria.title: Focus Visible
criteria.evidence: Dropdown type shows a 4px solid outline on :focus-visible. Outline uses --input-affix-focus-ring-color token. Text type has no focusable state. Focus suppression (outline:none without replacement) is caught by AFFIX-A11Y-003.

criteria.title: Name, Role, Value
criteria.evidence: Dropdown: role=button (implicit from <button>), name=text content of .input-affix__text, expanded state via aria-expanded. Text: role=presentation (div with no interactive role). Selected state via aria-selected requires parent listbox context — deferred to text-input assembly.

standards.name: Platform Design System Internal Standards
standards._note: Internal platform rules. IDs are stable across components.

criteria.title: Component must use design tokens for all visual values
criteria.evidence: All color, spacing, radius, and font values in input-affix.css reference --input-affix-* CSS custom properties with fallback values. Hardcoded value detection enforced by AFFIX-TOKEN-001.
criteria._note: Tokens are pending registration in token.css. Fallback values are present. Full token compliance pending.

criteria.title: RTL layout support is mandatory
criteria.evidence: input-affix.css uses padding-inline and gap. RTL layout handled by [dir="rtl"] on page ancestor. Physical padding properties (padding-left/right) are not used on the root. RTL audit enforced by AFFIX-RTL-001.

criteria.title: Semantic HTML element required
criteria.evidence: input-affix.contract.md specifies <button> for Dropdown type and <div> for Text type as the only valid root elements. AFFIX-STRUCT-001 enforces this.

criteria.title: Subcomponent must not be used in isolation
criteria.evidence: Documented in contract §10 (Assembly Context). input-affix must be used within a text-input host. AFFIX-USAGE-001 warns when affix appears without a sibling or parent input element.

criteria.title: Arabic language font support required
criteria.evidence: IBM Plex Sans Arabic is applied via --input-affix-font-family token. All text slots inherit this token.

criteria.title: Label language must match document language context
criteria.evidence: Cannot be verified automatically. Marked for manual review. Documented in input-affix.contract.md §9.

variant_compliance_notes.text-type (all variants): Text type has no interactive states. aria-hidden='true' is recommended to suppress redundant announcements when the label is already communicated via the connected input's label or placeholder.
variant_compliance_notes.dropdown-selected: WCAG 4.1.2: aria-selected='true' requires a parent element with role='listbox' or role='group' to be meaningful. This is provided by the text-input assembly.
variant_compliance_notes.disabled (all variants): WCAG 1.4.3 exempts inactive UI components. Low contrast of disabled state is intentional and compliant.
variant_compliance_notes.subtle-style (default state): Subtle Default has transparent background. WCAG 1.4.11 does not apply (no background or border). WCAG 1.4.3 still requires text to contrast at 4.5:1 against the actual background behind the affix.
variant_compliance_notes.focus-ring: Figma specifies border: 4px solid. Implementation uses outline: 4px solid to avoid layout shift. Visual result is identical; layout behavior differs.
